We're Your People™ — our service promise across NewBiz, Reactive, and Proactive service tracks. See the We're Your People™ 1-pager for the full framework.

2Health Care VIEW

Medicare status, supplement coverage, prescription drugs, provider network.
[Plan]
Medicare Plan Type
[Carrier]
Carrier
[$X]
Monthly Premium
[Plan]
Drug Coverage

Current Coverage

Populate: Medicare Part A/B/D status + effective date, Medigap/MAPD carrier + plan letter, supplement details, drug formulary fit, in-network providers, annual premium + deductible + MOOP.

AEP Review

Populate: Upcoming Annual Enrollment Period readiness. Is current plan still the best fit? Rate changes for next year? Drug formulary changes? Provider network changes? Any events (new diagnoses, new medications) that trigger a plan switch recommendation?

Health Care Costs — Current vs Projected

Populate: Current year OOP costs. Projected costs in retirement. Long-term care consideration trigger point. IRMAA (Income-Related Monthly Adjustment Amount) exposure based on tax bracket.

3Extended Care VIEW

Long-term care, chronic illness, aging in place, facility care readiness.
[$/day]
LTC Daily Benefit
[Term]
Benefit Period
[$X]
Lifetime Max
[Yes/No]
Inflation Rider

Existing LTC Policy

Populate: Carrier, policy #, issue date, elimination period, daily/monthly benefit, benefit period, inflation rider (simple vs compound, since when), facility vs home care coverage, premium + funding status, administrator contact (if serviced by third-party — e.g., Davies for COF LTC).

Coverage Gap Analysis

Populate: Current daily benefit vs current daily cost of care in client's state/county. Gap if any. Years of self-funding capacity before LTC policy kicks in. Years of policy coverage before self-funding resumes.

Aging in Place Strategy

Populate: Home modifications needed. Family/friend support network. Professional care team references. Facility preferences if needed. Funding sources layered (LTC insurance + annuity income + asset drawdown + family support).

Chronic Illness / Terminal Illness Riders

Populate: Existing life insurance policies with accelerated benefit riders. Income riders on annuities with LTC enhancements. Hybrid life/LTC products owned or candidates.

4Portfolio VIEW

Full asset picture across every account category. The complete financial snapshot.
[$Total]
Total Assets
[$NQ]
Non-Qualified
[$IRA]
Traditional IRA
[$Roth]
Roth IRA

Asset Allocation by Category

Populate: Banking (CDs, savings, checking). Annuities (fixed, indexed, variable, immediate). Life Insurance cash value. Investments (brokerage, advisory, retirement). Real estate (if tracked). Business interests (if applicable). Each with carrier/custodian, policy #, current value, tax status.

Risk Profile

Populate: Current risk exposure. % in guaranteed/principal-protected vs market-exposed. Rate sensitivity. Concentration risk. Liquidity ladder.

Sub-Account Detail

Populate: Reference link to Portfolio Detail tab (the full account-by-account table from Discovery).

5Accumulation VIEW

Growth assets, rates of return, tax-advantaged growth vehicles. What's still building.
[Rate]
Blended Yield
[$Growth]
Tax-Deferred
[$TaxFree]
Tax-Free
[$Taxable]
Taxable

Growth Engines

Populate: Each growth-oriented account with current rate, guaranteed rate, historical performance, upside cap, downside floor. Fixed index annuity participation rates. Whole life dividend history. CD renewal schedule.

Tax-Bucket Strategy

Populate: How assets are split across the three tax buckets: Taxable, Tax-Deferred, Tax-Free. Roth conversion opportunities. Tax-loss harvesting candidates. Capital gains timing.

Growth Assumptions for Planning

Populate: Conservative/Moderate/Optimistic projections. Rule-of-72 doubling timelines. Impact of fees and taxes on real returns.

6Income VIEW

Cash flow picture. Guaranteed income vs discretionary. Income gaps and surpluses.
[$/mo]
Monthly Income
[$/yr]
Annual Income
[Yrs]
Guaranteed Floor (yrs)
[$Surplus]
Surplus/(Gap)

Income Sources

Populate: Social Security (client + spouse), pension, SPIA income, annuity GLWB income, rental income, business income, part-time employment, investment yield. Monthly and annual amounts. Taxable portion of each.

Guaranteed vs Discretionary

Populate: What's contractually guaranteed (SS, pension, SPIA). What depends on market performance. What needs to be drawn from principal. Income replacement ratio vs pre-retirement income.

Cash Flow Needs

Populate: Fixed expenses (housing, food, health). Discretionary (travel, charitable, lifestyle). Known future expenses (grandchildren's college, weddings, home repairs). Emergency fund target.

RMD Income Impact

Populate: Projected RMDs at age 73+. Whether client NEEDS the RMD income or prefers it stay tax-deferred. QCD candidates for charitable giving.

7Estate VIEW

Legal documents, POA, trust, will, beneficiary status. The paperwork that controls the transfer.
[%]
Estate Plan Completeness
[Y/N]
Has Trust
[Y/N]
Has Will
[#]
Accounts Missing Beni

Estate Documents (6 Questions)

Populate from ProDashX EstateTab:
  1. Trust — exists? type (Revocable/Irrevocable/Testamentary), name, date
  2. Will — exists? type (Simple/Pour-Over/Testamentary), executor
  3. Financial POA — exists? name, durable or limited
  4. Healthcare POA — exists? name, scope
  5. Beneficiary Deed — exists? for which property
  6. Living Will / Advance Directive — exists? location of document

Beneficiary Audit

Populate: Every account with its primary + contingent beneficiary. Gap list (accounts with NO named beni = probate risk). Inconsistencies across accounts. Action items to close gaps.

Estate Tax Exposure

Populate: Total estate value vs federal estate tax exemption ($13.61M in 2026). State-specific inheritance/estate tax exposure (Iowa repealed 2025 — relevant for IA clients). Step-up in basis planning for appreciated assets.

Transfer Mechanisms

Populate: Named beneficiary transfers (annuities, life insurance, retirement accounts). POD/TOD designations (bank accounts). Deed-of-death transfers (real estate). Joint tenancy (if applicable). Trust distributions. Probate assets (the gap).

8Legacy VIEW

The transfer plan. Who gets what, when, how, and at what tax cost. The story behind the numbers.

Intended Beneficiaries

Populate: Who client wants to benefit. Family (children, grandchildren, siblings). Non-family (friends, partners). Charitable (church, foundations, causes). Equal vs proportional splits. Conditions on inheritances (age, milestones).

Transfer Timeline

Populate: Lifetime gifting (annual exclusion $18K in 2025, lifetime exemption). Death transfers. Deferred transfers (trust distributions). Charitable giving strategy (DAF, CRT, QCD).

Tax Cost to Heirs

Populate: Step-up in basis vs IRD (income in respect of decedent) on pre-tax accounts. Inherited IRA 10-year rule (SECURE Act 2.0) impact on non-spouse heirs. State inheritance tax if applicable.

Legacy Optimization Opportunities

Populate: Roth conversions to eliminate heirs' future RMDs. Life insurance as tax-free legacy vehicle. Charitable trusts. Family limited partnerships. Asset location strategy (what goes to which heir based on their tax situation).

The Legacy Story

RPI Engage Legacy™ — our framework for translating the numbers into a legacy story: Children • Care • Charity. See the RPI Engage Legacy™ 1-pager for the full framework.

SRPI Stage Matrix — Which VIEWs at Each Stage

The 8 VIEWs apply differently depending on the client's life stage. PROSPER, PRESERVE, PROTECT — each surfaces different priorities.
VIEW Purpose at Stage PROSPER PRESERVE PROTECT
1. Service Team Always shown — the people behind the plan ✓ ✓ ✓
2. Health Care Medicare becomes relevant at 65; before that it's group/ACA ◐ ✓ ✓
3. Extended Care LTC planning — optimal age to buy is 50s/60s, active in 70s+ ◐ ✓ ✓
4. Portfolio Always shown — the full snapshot of what's there ✓ ✓ ✓
5. Accumulation Dominant focus in PROSPER, tapers in PROTECT ✓ ✓ ◐
6. Income Becomes dominant at retirement ◐ ✓ ✓
7. Estate Documents should exist in all stages; urgency increases with age ◐ ✓ ✓
8. Legacy Strategic focus in PROTECT; present but secondary in PRESERVE — ✓ ✓
Legend: ✓ Core view for this stage  •  ◐ Optional/contextual  •  — Not typically surfaced

Stage Definitions

  • PROSPER — Accumulation phase. Client is building wealth, still working, growth is the priority. Typical age: 30s-50s.
  • PRESERVE — Transition phase. Client is at or near retirement, protecting what they've built while still growing select assets. Income planning becomes critical. Typical age: 55-75.
  • PROTECT — Distribution and legacy phase. Client is drawing income, focused on transfer strategy, estate completeness, and charitable intent. Typical age: 70+.
RPI PRESERVE Stage Analysis Template • v1 (2026-04-10) • Registered by VOLTRON CSO
[Client Name] • [Meeting Date] • Prepared by [Advisor] • #RunningOurOwnRACE
